evidence.rule_summary_from_author: C_PIN rows represent password credentials. C_PIN UID column 0x00 SHALL NOT be modifiable by the host. For C_PIN objects that exist at issuance, Name column 0x01 and CommonName column 0x02 SHALL NOT be modifiable by the host. Opal preconfigures Admin SP C_PIN_SID, C_PIN_MSID, C_PIN_Admin1 rows and Locking SP C_PIN_Admin1 and C_PIN_User1 rows at issuance, so successful host Set of these columns is invalid.

source_snippets.text: 5.3.2.12 Credential Table Group - C_PIN (Object Table) The C_PIN table contains one row for each password credential. The C_PIN object with UID=0x00 0x00 0x00 0x0B 0x00 0x00 0x00 0x01 and Name=“SID” is the default SID object. Table 181 C_PIN Table Description ```markdown | Column Number | Column Name | IsUnique | Column Type | |---------------|-------------|----------|---------------------| | 0x00 | UID | | uid | | 0x01 | Name | Yes | name | | 0x02 | CommonName | Yes | name | | 0x03 | PIN | | password | | 0x04 | CharSet | | byte_table_ref | | 0x05 | TryLimit | | uinteger_4 | | 0x06 | Tries | | uinteger_4 | | 0x07 | Persistence | | boolean | ```
5.3.2.12.1 UID This is the unique identifier of this row in the C_PIN table. This column SHALL NOT be modifiable by the host.
5.3.2.12.2 Name This is the name of the C_PIN object. For C_PIN objects that exist at issuance, this column SHALL NOT be modifiable by the host.
5.3.2.12.3 CommonName This is a name that MAY be shared by multiple C_PIN objects. For C_PIN objects that exist at issuance, this column SHALL NOT be modifiable by the host.

4.2.1.8 C_PIN (M) The C_PIN table is defined in [2], and Table 25 defines the Preconfiguration Data for the C_PIN table. Table 25 - Admin SP - C_PIN Table Preconfiguration ```json [ { "UID": "00 00 00 0B 00 00 00 01", "Name": "C_PIN_SID", "CommonName": "", "PIN": "VU", "CharSet": "Null", "TryLimit": "VU", "Tries": "VU", "Persistence": "FALSE" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 0B 00 00 84 02", "Name": "C_PIN_MSID", "CommonName": "", "PIN": "MSID", "CharSet": "", "TryLimit": "", "Tries": "", "Persistence": "" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 0B 00 00 02 01", "Name": "C_PIN_Admin1", "CommonName": "", "PIN": "-", "CharSet": "Null", "TryLimit": "0", "Tries": "0", "Persistence": "FALSE" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 0B 00 00 02 00 (+XX)", "Name": "C_PIN_AdminXX", "CommonName": "", "PIN": "-", "CharSet": "Null", "TryLimit": "0", "Tries": "0", "Persistence": "FALSE" } ] ``` For Storage Devices that will be used in environments where an automated take ownership process is required, the initial PIN column value of C_PIN_SID SHALL be set to the PIN column value of C_PIN_MSID. In order to allow for alternative take ownership processes, the initial PIN column value of C_PIN_SID MAY be Vendor Unique (VU). Start of Informative Comment Several activation / take ownership models are possible. The simplest model, which is the only model supported by Opal v1.00, is a process whereby the host discovers the initial C_PIN_SID PIN value by performing a Get operation on the C_PIN_MSID object. This model requires that the initial C_PIN_SID PIN be the value of the C_PIN_MSID PIN. Opal v2.00 allows the initial C_PIN_SID PIN value to be vendor unique in order to allow for alternative activation / take ownership models. Such models require that the C_PIN_SID PIN be retrieved in a way that is beyond the scope of this specification. Before a device vendor chooses to implement an activation / take ownership model based on a vendor unique SID PIN, the Storage Device vendor must undertake due diligence to ensure that the ecosystem exists to support such an activation / take ownership model. Having a C_PIN_SID PIN value that is different from the C_PIN_MSID PIN value may have serious ramifications, such as the inability to take ownership of the Storage Device. See section 5.1.2.2.1 for an explanation of how the Revert method affects the value of the C_PIN_SID PIN column. End of Informative Comment

4.3.1.9 C_PIN (M) Table 41 includes Optional rows designated with (O) Notes: 1. If the Locking SP's original life cycle state is Manufactured-Inactive, see 5.1.1.2 for the initial value of C_PIN_Admin1.PIN. If the Locking SP's original life cycle state is Manufactured, then the initial value of C_PIN_Admin1.PIN is the same as the Admin SP's C_PIN_MSID.PIN value. Table 41 - Locking SP - C_PIN Table Preconfiguration ```json [ { "UID": "00 00 00 0B 00 01 00 01", "Name": "C_PIN_Admin1", "CommonName": "", "PIN": "SID or MSID (1)", "CharSet": "Null", "TryLimit": 0, "Tries": 0, "Persistence": "FALSE" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 0B 00 01 00 02", "Name": "C_PIN_Admin2", "CommonName": "", "PIN": "\"\"", "CharSet": "Null", "TryLimit": 0, "Tries": 0, "Persistence": "FALSE" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 0B 00 01 00 03", "Name": "C_PIN_Admin3", "CommonName": "", "PIN": "\"\"", "CharSet": "Null", "TryLimit": 0, "Tries": 0, "Persistence": "FALSE" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 0B 00 01 00 04", "Name": "C_PIN_Admin4", "CommonName": "", "PIN": "\"\"", "CharSet": "Null", "TryLimit": 0, "Tries": 0, "Persistence": "FALSE" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 0B 00 01 00 00 (+XX XX)", "Name": "C_PIN_AdminXXXX", "CommonName": "", "PIN": "\"\"", "CharSet": "Null", "TryLimit": 0, "Tries": 0, "Persistence": "FALSE" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 0B 00 03 00 01", "Name": "C_PIN_User1", "CommonName": "", "PIN": "\"\"", "CharSet": "Null", "TryLimit": 0, "Tries": 0, "Persistence": "FALSE" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 0B 00 03 00 00 (+MM MM)", "Name": "C_PIN_UserMMMM", "CommonName": "", "PIN": "\"\"", "CharSet": "Null", "TryLimit": 0, "Tries": 0, "Persistence": "FALSE" } ] ```

For C_PIN objects that exist at issuance, Name column 0x01 and CommonName column 0x02 SHALL NOT be modifiable by the host. Opal preconfigures Admin SP C_PIN_SID, C_PIN_MSID, C_PIN_Admin1 rows and Locking SP C_PIN_Admin1 and C_PIN_User1 rows at issuance, so successful host Set of these columns is invalid.

source_snippets.text: 5.3.2.12 Credential Table Group - C_PIN (Object Table) The C_PIN table contains one row for each password credential. The C_PIN object with UID=0x00 0x00 0x00 0x0B 0x00 0x00 0x00 0x01 and Name=“SID” is the default SID object. Table 181 C_PIN Table Description ```markdown | Column Number | Column Name | IsUnique | Column Type | |---------------|-------------|----------|---------------------| | 0x00 | UID | | uid | | 0x01 | Name | Yes | name | | 0x02 | CommonName | Yes | name | | 0x03 | PIN | | password | | 0x04 | CharSet | | byte_table_ref | | 0x05 | TryLimit | | uinteger_4 | | 0x06 | Tries | | uinteger_4 | | 0x07 | Persistence | | boolean | ```
5.3.2.12.1 UID This is the unique identifier of this row in the C_PIN table. This column SHALL NOT be modifiable by the host.
5.3.2.12.2 Name This is the name of the C_PIN object. For C_PIN objects that exist at issuance, this column SHALL NOT be modifiable by the host.
5.3.2.12.3 CommonName This is a name that MAY be shared by multiple C_PIN objects. For C_PIN objects that exist at issuance, this column SHALL NOT be modifiable by the host.

4.2.1.8 C_PIN (M) The C_PIN table is defined in [2], and Table 25 defines the Preconfiguration Data for the C_PIN table. Table 25 - Admin SP - C_PIN Table Preconfiguration ```json [ { "UID": "00 00 00 0B 00 00 00 01", "Name": "C_PIN_SID", "CommonName": "", "PIN": "VU", "CharSet": "Null", "TryLimit": "VU", "Tries": "VU", "Persistence": "FALSE" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 0B 00 00 84 02", "Name": "C_PIN_MSID", "CommonName": "", "PIN": "MSID", "CharSet": "", "TryLimit": "", "Tries": "", "Persistence": "" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 0B 00 00 02 01", "Name": "C_PIN_Admin1", "CommonName": "", "PIN": "-", "CharSet": "Null", "TryLimit": "0", "Tries": "0", "Persistence": "FALSE" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 0B 00 00 02 00 (+XX)", "Name": "C_PIN_AdminXX", "CommonName": "", "PIN": "-", "CharSet": "Null", "TryLimit": "0", "Tries": "0", "Persistence": "FALSE" } ] ``` For Storage Devices that will be used in environments where an automated take ownership process is required, the initial PIN column value of C_PIN_SID SHALL be set to the PIN column value of C_PIN_MSID. In order to allow for alternative take ownership processes, the initial PIN column value of C_PIN_SID MAY be Vendor Unique (VU). Start of Informative Comment Several activation / take ownership models are possible. The simplest model, which is the only model supported by Opal v1.00, is a process whereby the host discovers the initial C_PIN_SID PIN value by performing a Get operation on the C_PIN_MSID object. This model requires that the initial C_PIN_SID PIN be the value of the C_PIN_MSID PIN. Opal v2.00 allows the initial C_PIN_SID PIN value to be vendor unique in order to allow for alternative activation / take ownership models. Such models require that the C_PIN_SID PIN be retrieved in a way that is beyond the scope of this specification. Before a device vendor chooses to implement an activation / take ownership model based on a vendor unique SID PIN, the Storage Device vendor must undertake due diligence to ensure that the ecosystem exists to support such an activation / take ownership model. Having a C_PIN_SID PIN value that is different from the C_PIN_MSID PIN value may have serious ramifications, such as the inability to take ownership of the Storage Device. See section 5.1.2.2.1 for an explanation of how the Revert method affects the value of the C_PIN_SID PIN column. End of Informative Comment

4.3.1.9 C_PIN (M) Table 41 includes Optional rows designated with (O) Notes: 1. If the Locking SP's original life cycle state is Manufactured-Inactive, see 5.1.1.2 for the initial value of C_PIN_Admin1.PIN. If the Locking SP's original life cycle state is Manufactured, then the initial value of C_PIN_Admin1.PIN is the same as the Admin SP's C_PIN_MSID.PIN value. Table 41 - Locking SP - C_PIN Table Preconfiguration ```json [ { "UID": "00 00 00 0B 00 01 00 01", "Name": "C_PIN_Admin1", "CommonName": "", "PIN": "SID or MSID (1)", "CharSet": "Null", "TryLimit": 0, "Tries": 0, "Persistence": "FALSE" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 0B 00 01 00 02", "Name": "C_PIN_Admin2", "CommonName": "", "PIN": "\"\"", "CharSet": "Null", "TryLimit": 0, "Tries": 0, "Persistence": "FALSE" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 0B 00 01 00 03", "Name": "C_PIN_Admin3", "CommonName": "", "PIN": "\"\"", "CharSet": "Null", "TryLimit": 0, "Tries": 0, "Persistence": "FALSE" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 0B 00 01 00 04", "Name": "C_PIN_Admin4", "CommonName": "", "PIN": "\"\"", "CharSet": "Null", "TryLimit": 0, "Tries": 0, "Persistence": "FALSE" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 0B 00 01 00 00 (+XX XX)", "Name": "C_PIN_AdminXXXX", "CommonName": "", "PIN": "\"\"", "CharSet": "Null", "TryLimit": 0, "Tries": 0, "Persistence": "FALSE" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 0B 00 03 00 01", "Name": "C_PIN_User1", "CommonName": "", "PIN": "\"\"", "CharSet": "Null", "TryLimit": 0, "Tries": 0, "Persistence": "FALSE" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 0B 00 03 00 00 (+MM MM)", "Name": "C_PIN_UserMMMM", "CommonName": "", "PIN": "\"\"", "CharSet": "Null", "TryLimit": 0, "Tries": 0, "Persistence": "FALSE" } ] ```
